overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions unanimously and voted to recommend a limited fee waiver for tornado-damaged properties, restricting it to only septic system reconnect fees rather than the broader waiver initially proposed by staff. The committee also received educational presentations from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Rezoning petition 5A1: Tim Veland on behalf of James Offer and David Trait - Urban Residential and General Agriculture to Rural Residential in Town of Green Valley
Rezoning petition 5A2: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz - Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture in Town of McMillan
Consideration of fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Educational presentation: Solid Waste Department overview
Educational presentation: Parks, Recreation and Forestry Department overview
Educational presentation: Conservation Planning and Zoning Department overview

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes with Supervisor Drebeck seconding. The motion carried with no opposition on a voice vote.

discussions.item: Rezoning petition 5A1: James Offer and David Trait property
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning approximately 2 acres from Urban Residential and General Agriculture to Rural Residential in the Town of Green Valley. The Town Board recommended approval on March 10, 2026. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact and conclusions of law, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. The motion carried unanimously with no public comment offered.

discussions.item: Rezoning petition 5A2: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz property
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture in the Town of McMillan. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature of the property; Pogle explained it currently has a home and horse riding stable, and the rezoning would make the home a conforming use rather than non-conforming. No natural areas would be lost. The Town Board recommended approval on April 20, 2026. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, seconded by Supervisor Drew. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waiver for April 17, 2026 tornado-damaged properties
discussions.body: Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and septic system fees for properties damaged by the April 17th tornado affecting Towns of Ringle, Easton, and villages of Kronenwetter and Weston. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future disasters. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concern about fairness to property owners hit by disasters not declared emergencies. Marilyn Bend asked about septic hookup costs. After debate, Supervisor Drebeck moved to amend the proposal to only waive reconnect fees ($160) for septic systems, not other zoning or septic fees. The amendment was seconded by Supervisor Kurt and passed. The amended resolution was then approved on a non-unanimous voice vote to forward to the county board.

Director David Hagenbusher provided an overview of landfill operations, explaining the facility has operated since 1980 and accepts about 1,000 tons daily. He highlighted the 2023 partnership with Vidi Energy to convert landfill gas to renewable natural gas, the $6 million 11-acre expansion starting this year, and the requirement by 2032 to install on-site leachate treatment due to PFAS concerns. Current hauling costs are approximately $90,000 per month.

County Forest Administrator Tom Lovelin presented on behalf of Director Jamie, covering the department's 44 full-time and 120 seasonal employees serving both county and city facilities. He noted Nine Mile County Forest generates $3.25 million annually for the local economy from out-of-town visitors. Supervisor Sefeld announced Tom Lovelin is retiring June 1st after nearly 30 years of service, with Joe Tucker selected as his replacement.

publicComment: No public comment was offered. No one signed up for public comment at the beginning of the meeting, and no members of the public spoke during the public hearing portions for either rezoning petition.
actionItems: Forward rezoning approval for James Offer and David Trait property (Urban Residential/General Agriculture to Rural Residential) to County Board
Forward rezoning approval for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz property (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture) to County Board

Forward amended resolution to County Board recommending waiver of only septic system reconnect fees ($160) for properties damaged by April 17, 2026 tornado, with one-year duration from county board approval
Town of Green Valley and Town of McMillan should update comprehensive plans to reflect zoning changes if applicable
CPZ staff to track costs and requests related to tornado fee waiver program
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
